   409→   409→   409→   409→async def _refresh_search_results(frame: Frame) -> None:
   410→   410→   410→   410→    """Click "换一换" button in the search iframe to load new candidates.
   411→   411→   411→   411→
   412→   412→   412→   412→    Boss直聘 search does NOT use infinite scroll. Instead it shows ~30 cards
   413→   413→   413→   413→    and provides a "换一换" button to refresh the list with new candidates.
   414→   414→   414→   414→    """
   415→   415→   415→   415→    try:
   416→   416→   416→   416→        refresh_btn = await frame.query_selector('.change-btn')
   417→   417→   417→   417→        if refresh_btn and await refresh_btn.is_visible():
   418→   418→   418→   418→            await refresh_btn.click()
   419→   419→   419→   419→            # Wait for new cards to load
   420→   420→   420→   420→            await frame.wait_for_timeout(2000)
   421→   421→   421→   421→            return
   422→   422→   422→   422→    except Exception as e:
   423→   423→   423→   423→        logger.warning(f"Could not click refresh button: {e}")
   424→   424→   424→   424→
   425→   425→   425→   425→    # Fallback: scroll the iframe body to trigger any lazy loading
   426→   426→   426→   426→    await frame.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)")
   427→   427→   427→   427→    await frame.wait_for_timeout(1500)
   428→   428→   428→   428→
   429→   429→   429→   429→
   430→   430→   430→   430→async def _check_quota_exhausted(frame: Frame) -> bool:
   431→   431→   431→   431→    """Check if Boss直聘 shows a quota exhaustion indicator."""
   432→   432→   432→   432→    try:
   433→   433→   433→   433→        result = await frame.evaluate("""
   434→   434→   434→   434→            () => {
   435→   435→   435→   435→                const bodyText = document.body ? document.body.innerText : '';
   436→   436→   436→   436→                const quotaIndicators = [
   437→   437→   437→   437→                    '今日沟通次数已用完',
   438→   438→   438→   438→                    '沟通次数已达上限',
   439→   439→   439→   439→                    '今日联系人数已满',
   440→   440→   440→   440→                    '升级会员',
   441→   441→   441→   441→                    '开通会员享更多沟通'
   442→   442→   442→   442→                ];
   443→   443→   443→   443→                for (const indicator of quotaIndicators) {
   444→   444→   444→   444→                    if (bodyText.includes(indicator)) {
   445→   445→   445→   445→                        return true;
   446→   446→   446→   446→                    }
   447→   447→   447→   447→                }
   448→   448→   448→   448→                return false;
   449→   449→   449→   449→            }
   450→   450→   450→   450→        """)
   451→   451→   451→   451→        return result
   452→   452→   452→   452→    except Exception:
   453→   453→   453→   453→        return False
   454→   454→   454→   454→
   455→   455→   455→   455→
   456→   456→   456→   456→def _save_candidates_to_db(
   457→   457→   457→   457→    candidates: List[CandidateBasic],
   458→   458→   458→   458→    keywords: List[str],
   459→   459→   459→   459→    db_manager,
   460→   460→   460→   460→) -> None:
   461→   461→   461→   461→    """Save or update candidates in the database."""
   462→   462→   462→   462→    source = ', '.join(keywords)
   463→   463→   463→   463→
   464→   464→   464→   464→    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   465→   465→   465→   465→        # Batch-fetch existing candidates to avoid N+1 queries
   466→   466→   466→   466→        boss_ids = [c.boss_id for c in candidates]
   467→   467→   467→   467→        existing_map = {}
   468→   468→   468→   468→        for i in range(0, len(boss_ids), 500):
   469→   469→   469→   469→            chunk = boss_ids[i:i + 500]
   470→   470→   470→   470→            rows = session.query(Candidate).filter(
   471→   471→   471→   471→                Candidate.boss_id.in_(chunk)
   472→   472→   472→   472→            ).all()
   473→   473→   473→   473→            for row in rows:
   474→   474→   474→   474→                existing_map[row.boss_id] = row
   475→   475→   475→   475→
   476→   476→   476→   476→        for c in candidates:
   477→   477→   477→   477→            existing = existing_map.get(c.boss_id)
   478→   478→   478→   478→            if existing:
   479→   479→   479→   479→                existing.name = c.name or existing.name
   480→   480→   480→   480→                existing.title = c.title or existing.title
   481→   481→   481→   481→                existing.company = c.company or existing.company
   482→   482→   482→   482→                existing.location = c.location or existing.location
   483→   483→   483→   483→                existing.salary_expect = c.salary_expect or existing.salary_expect
   484→   484→   484→   484→                existing.work_years = c.work_years or existing.work_years
   485→   485→   485→   485→                existing.education_level = c.education_level or existing.education_level
   486→   486→   486→   486→                existing.profile_url = c.profile_url or existing.profile_url
   487→   487→   487→   487→                if source and source not in (existing.source_search or ''):
   488→   488→   488→   488→                    existing.source_search = f"{existing.source_search or ''}, {source}".strip(', ')
   489→   489→   489→   489→            else:
   490→   490→   490→   490→                new_candidate = Candidate(
   491→   491→   491→   491→                    boss_id=c.boss_id,
   492→   492→   492→   492→                    name=c.name,
   493→   493→   493→   493→                    title=c.title,
   494→   494→   494→   494→                    company=c.company,
   495→   495→   495→   495→                    location=c.location,
   496→   496→   496→   496→                    salary_expect=c.salary_expect,
   497→   497→   497→   497→                    work_years=c.work_years,
   498→   498→   498→   498→                    education_level=c.education_level,
   499→   499→   499→   499→                    profile_url=c.profile_url,
   500→   500→   500→   500→                    source_search=source,
   501→   501→   501→   501→                )
   502→   502→   502→   502→                session.add(new_candidate)
   503→   503→   503→   503→
   504→   504→   504→   504→
   505→   505→   505→   505→def _display_results(candidates: List[CandidateBasic], keywords: List[str]) -> None:
   506→   506→   506→   506→    """Display search results in a Rich table."""
   507→   507→   507→   507→    if not candidates:
   508→   508→   508→   508→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates found.[/yellow]")
   509→   509→   509→   509→        return
   510→   510→   510→   510→
   511→   511→   511→   511→    table = Table(title=f"Search Results ({len(candidates)} candidates)")
   512→   512→   512→   512→    table.add_column("Boss ID", style="dim", max_width=20)
   513→   513→   513→   513→    table.add_column("Name", style="bold")
   514→   514→   514→   514→    table.add_column("Title")
   515→   515→   515→   515→    table.add_column("Company")
   516→   516→   516→   516→    table.add_column("Education")
   517→   517→   517→   517→    table.add_column("Salary")
   518→   518→   518→   518→    table.add_column("Experience")
   519→   519→   519→   519→    table.add_column("Active", style="green")
   520→   520→   520→   520→
   521→   521→   521→   521→    for c in candidates[:50]:
   522→   522→   522→   522→        table.add_row(
   523→   523→   523→   523→            c.boss_id[:20],
   524→   524→   524→   524→            c.name,
   525→   525→   525→   525→            c.title[:30] if c.title else "",
   526→   526→   526→   526→            c.company[:20] if c.company else "",
   527→   527→   527→   527→            c.education_level or "",
   528→   528→   528→   528→            c.salary_expect or "",
   529→   529→   529→   529→            c.work_years or "",
   530→   530→   530→   530→            c.active_status or "",
   531→   531→   531→   531→        )
   532→   532→   532→   532→
   533→   533→   533→   533→    console.print(table)
   534→   534→   534→   534→
   535→   535→   535→   535→    if len(candidates) > 50:
   536→   536→   536→   536→        console.print(f"[dim]...and {len(candidates) - 50} more candidates[/dim]")

1→     1→     1→"""Random delay generators for human-like behavior simulation."""
     2→     2→     2→
     3→     3→     3→import math
     4→     4→     4→import random
     5→     5→     5→import asyncio
     6→     6→     6→import time
     7→     7→     7→from loguru import logger
     8→     8→     8→from src.utils.config import get_config
     9→     9→     9→
    10→    10→    10→
    11→    11→    11→_session_start: float = time.monotonic()
    12→    12→    12→_action_count: int = 0
    13→    13→    13→
    14→    14→    14→
    15→    15→    15→def reset_session():
    16→    16→    16→    global _session_start, _action_count
    17→    17→    17→    _session_start = time.monotonic()
    18→    18→    18→    _action_count = 0
    19→    19→    19→
    20→    20→    20→
    21→    21→    21→def _lognormal_delay(min_val: float, max_val: float) -> float:
    22→    22→    22→    mid = (min_val + max_val) / 2
    23→    23→    23→    mu = math.log(mid)
    24→    24→    24→    sigma = 0.4
    25→    25→    25→    delay = random.lognormvariate(mu, sigma)
    26→    26→    26→    return max(min_val, min(delay, max_val * 1.5))
    27→    27→    27→
    28→    28→    28→
    29→    29→    29→def _fatigue_multiplier() -> float:
    30→    30→    30→    elapsed_minutes = (time.monotonic() - _session_start) / 60.0
    31→    31→    31→    if elapsed_minutes < 10:
    32→    32→    32→        return 1.0
    33→    33→    33→    factor = 1.0 + 0.006 * min(elapsed_minutes - 10, 100)
    34→    34→    34→    return min(factor, 1.6)
    35→    35→    35→
    36→    36→    36→
    37→    37→    37→def _maybe_distraction_pause() -> float:
    38→    38→    38→    global _action_count
    39→    39→    39→    _action_count += 1
    40→    40→    40→    if _action_count < 5:
    41→    41→    41→        return 0.0
    42→    42→    42→    if random.random() < 0.03:
    43→    43→    43→        pause = random.uniform(5.0, 30.0)
    44→    44→    44→        logger.debug(f"Simulating distraction pause: {pause:.1f}s")
    45→    45→    45→        return pause
    46→    46→    46→    return 0.0
    47→    47→    47→
    48→    48→    48→
    49→    49→    49→def _time_of_day_multiplier() -> float:
    50→    50→    50→    import datetime
    51→    51→    51→    hour = datetime.datetime.now().hour
    52→    52→    52→    if 23 <= hour or hour < 7:
    53→    53→    53→        return 1.3
    54→    54→    54→    elif (10 <= hour <= 12) or (14 <= hour <= 17):
    55→    55→    55→        return 1.0
    56→    56→    56→    else:
    57→    57→    57→        return 1.1
    58→    58→    58→
    59→    59→    59→
    60→    60→    60→def get_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action') -> float:
    61→    61→    61→    config = get_config()
    62→    62→    62→
    63→    63→    63→    delay_ranges = {
    64→    64→    64→        'page_load': (
    65→    65→    65→            config.get('delays.page_load_min', 2),
    66→    66→    66→            config.get('delays.page_load_max', 5)
    67→    67→    67→        ),
    68→    68→    68→        'action': (
    69→    69→    69→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
    70→    70→    70→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
    71→    71→    71→        ),
    72→    72→    72→        'typing': (
    73→    73→    73→            config.get('delays.typing_min', 0.05),
    74→    74→    74→            config.get('delays.typing_max', 0.15)
    75→    75→    75→        ),
    76→    76→    76→        'scroll': (
    77→    77→    77→            config.get('delays.scroll_min', 0.5),
    78→    78→    78→            config.get('delays.scroll_max', 1.5)
    79→    79→    79→        ),
    80→    80→    80→        'message': (
    81→    81→    81→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
    82→    82→    82→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
    83→    83→    83→        ),
    84→    84→    84→    }
    85→    85→    85→
    86→    86→    86→    min_delay, max_delay = delay_ranges.get(delay_type, (0.5, 2.0))
    87→    87→    87→    base_delay = _lognormal_delay(min_delay, max_delay)
    88→    88→    88→
    89→    89→    89→    if delay_type != 'typing':
    90→    90→    90→        base_delay *= _fatigue_multiplier()
    91→    91→    91→        base_delay *= _time_of_day_multiplier()
    92→    92→    92→
    93→    93→    93→    if delay_type in ('action', 'page_load', 'message'):
    94→    94→    94→        base_delay += _maybe_distraction_pause()
    95→    95→    95→
    96→    96→    96→    return base_delay
    97→    97→    97→
    98→    98→    98→
    99→    99→    99→async def async_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action'):
   100→   100→   100→    delay = get_random_delay(delay_type)
   101→   101→   101→    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   102→   102→   102→

   133→   133→   133→   133→
   134→   134→   134→   134→
   135→   135→   135→   135→class DatabaseManager:
   136→   136→   136→   136→    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""
   137→   137→   137→   137→
   138→   138→   138→   138→    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
   139→   139→   139→   139→        if db_path is None:
   140→   140→   140→   140→            config = get_config()
   141→   141→   141→   141→            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/boss.db')
   142→   142→   142→   142→
   143→   143→   143→   143→        db_file = Path(db_path)
   144→   144→   144→   144→        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   145→   145→   145→   145→
   146→   146→   146→   146→        self.db_path = db_path
   147→   147→   147→   147→        # NullPool: each session gets a fresh connection, safe for
   148→   148→   148→   148→        # concurrent Web + background task access to SQLite.
   149→   149→   149→   149→        self.engine = create_engine(
   150→   150→   150→   150→            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
   151→   151→   151→   151→            echo=False,
   152→   152→   152→   152→            poolclass=NullPool,
   153→   153→   153→   153→            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
   154→   154→   154→   154→        )
   155→   155→   155→   155→        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
   156→   156→   156→   156→
   157→   157→   157→   157→        self.init_db()
   158→   158→   158→   158→        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")
   159→   159→   159→   159→
   160→   160→   160→   160→    def init_db(self):
   161→   161→   161→   161→        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
   162→   162→   162→   162→        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
   163→   163→   163→   163→        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")
   164→   164→   164→   164→
   165→   165→   165→   165→        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
   166→   166→   166→   166→        run_migrations(self.engine)
   167→   167→   167→   167→
   168→   168→   168→   168→    @contextmanager
   169→   169→   169→   169→    def session_scope(self):
   170→   170→   170→   170→        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
   171→   171→   171→   171→        session = self.Session()
   172→   172→   172→   172→        try:
   173→   173→   173→   173→            yield session
   174→   174→   174→   174→            session.commit()
   175→   175→   175→   175→        except Exception:
   176→   176→   176→   176→            session.rollback()
   177→   177→   177→   177→            raise
   178→   178→   178→   178→        finally:
   179→   179→   179→   179→            session.close()
   180→   180→   180→   180→
   181→   181→   181→   181→    def drop_all(self):
   182→   182→   182→   182→        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
   183→   183→   183→   183→        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
   184→   184→   184→   184→        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")
   185→   185→   185→   185→
   186→   186→   186→   186→
   187→   187→   187→   187→# Global database manager instance
   188→   188→   188→   188→_db_manager = None
   189→   189→   189→   189→
   190→   190→   190→   190→
   191→   191→   191→   191→def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
   192→   192→   192→   192→    """Get global database manager instance."""
   193→   193→   193→   193→    global _db_manager
   194→   194→   194→   194→    if _db_manager is None:
   195→   195→   195→   195→        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
   196→   196→   196→   196→    return _db_manager
   197→   197→   197→   197→
   198→   198→   198→   198→
   199→   199→   199→   199→def reset_db_manager() -> None:
   200→   200→   200→   200→    """Reset global database manager (for testing)."""
   201→   201→   201→   201→    global _db_manager
   202→   202→   202→   202→    _db_manager = None
   203→   203→   203→   203→

1→     1→     1→"""Chrome process lifecycle manager for Web service.
     2→     2→     2→
     3→     3→     3→Manages starting, health-checking, and restarting the Chrome browser
     4→     4→     4→process with CDP enabled. Used by the Web service lifespan to ensure
     5→     5→     5→Chrome is always available for browser automation tasks.
     6→     6→     6→"""
     7→     7→     7→
     8→     8→     8→import asyncio
     9→     9→     9→import shutil
    10→    10→    10→import signal
    11→    11→    11→import subprocess
    12→    12→    12→import sys
    13→    13→    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→    14→    14→from typing import Optional
    15→    15→    15→
    16→    16→    16→import httpx
    17→    17→    17→from loguru import logger
    18→    18→    18→
    19→    19→    19→from src.utils.config import get_config
    20→    20→    20→
    21→    21→    21→
    22→    22→    22→class ChromeProcessManager:
    23→    23→    23→    """Manages the Chrome browser process for the Web service.
    24→    24→    24→
    25→    25→    25→    - Auto-starts Chrome in headed mode with --remote-debugging-port
    26→    26→    26→    - Periodically health-checks via CDP HTTP endpoint
    27→    27→    27→    - Auto-restarts Chrome if it crashes
    28→    28→    28→    - Gracefully terminates Chrome on shutdown
    29→    29→    29→    """
    30→    30→    30→
    31→    31→    31→    def __init__(self):
    32→    32→    32→        self._config = get_config()
    33→    33→    33→        self._process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
    34→    34→    34→        self._health_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
    35→    35→    35→        self._running = False
    36→    36→    36→
    37→    37→    37→    @property
    38→    38→    38→    def debug_port(self) -> int:
    39→    39→    39→        return self._config.get('web.chrome_debug_port', 9222)
    40→    40→    40→
    41→    41→    41→    @property
    42→    42→    42→    def user_data_dir(self) -> str:
    43→    43→    43→        return self._config.get('web.chrome_user_data_dir', 'data/.chrome-profile')
    44→    44→    44→
    45→    45→    45→    @property
    46→    46→    46→    def cdp_url(self) -> str:
    47→    47→    47→        return f'http://127.0.0.1:{self.debug_port}'
    48→    48→    48→
    49→    49→    49→    @property
    50→    50→    50→    def is_chrome_running(self) -> bool:
    51→    51→    51→        return self._process is not None and self._process.poll() is None
    52→    52→    52→
    53→    53→    53→    def _find_chrome_binary(self) -> str:
    54→    54→    54→        """Find Chrome binary on the current platform."""
    55→    55→    55→        if sys.platform == 'darwin':
    56→    56→    56→            candidates = [
    57→    57→    57→                '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome',
    58→    58→    58→                '/Applications/Chromium.app/Contents/MacOS/Chromium',
    59→    59→    59→            ]
    60→    60→    60→        elif sys.platform == 'win32':
    61→    61→    61→            candidates = [
    62→    62→    62→                r'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
    63→    63→    63→                r'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
    64→    64→    64→            ]
    65→    65→    65→        else:
    66→    66→    66→            candidates = [
    67→    67→    67→                'google-chrome',
    68→    68→    68→                'google-chrome-stable',
    69→    69→    69→                'chromium',
    70→    70→    70→                'chromium-browser',
    71→    71→    71→            ]
    72→    72→    72→
    73→    73→    73→        for candidate in candidates:
    74→    74→    74→            if Path(candidate).exists() or shutil.which(candidate):
    75→    75→    75→                return candidate
    76→    76→    76→
    77→    77→    77→        raise RuntimeError(
    78→    78→    78→            'Chrome binary not found. Install Google Chrome or set the path manually.'
    79→    79→    79→        )
    80→    80→    80→
    81→    81→    81→    async def _check_cdp_health(self) -> bool:
    82→    82→    82→        """Check if Chrome CDP endpoint is responsive."""
    83→    83→    83→        try:
    84→    84→    84→            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
    85→    85→    85→                resp = await client.get(
    86→    86→    86→                    f'{self.cdp_url}/json/version',
    87→    87→    87→                    timeout=5.0,
    88→    88→    88→                )
    89→    89→    89→                return resp.status_code == 200
    90→    90→    90→        except Exception:
    91→    91→    91→            return False
    92→    92→    92→
    93→    93→    93→    def _start_chrome_process(self) -> None:
    94→    94→    94→        """Start Chrome with CDP enabled."""
    95→    95→    95→        chrome_binary = self._find_chrome_binary()
    96→    96→    96→        user_data = Path(self.user_data_dir).resolve()
    97→    97→    97→        user_data.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    98→    98→    98→
    99→    99→    99→        args = [
   100→   100→   100→            chrome_binary,
   101→   101→   101→            f'--remote-debugging-port={self.debug_port}',
   102→   102→   102→            f'--user-data-dir={user_data}',
   103→   103→   103→            '--no-first-run',
   104→   104→   104→            '--no-default-browser-check',
   105→   105→   105→            '--disable-background-timer-throttling',
   106→   106→   106→            '--disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows',
   107→   107→   107→            '--disable-renderer-backgrounding',
   108→   108→   108→        ]
   109→   109→   109→
   110→   110→   110→        logger.info(f'Starting Chrome: {" ".join(args[:3])}...')
   111→   111→   111→        self._process = subprocess.Popen(
   112→   112→   112→            args,
   113→   113→   113→            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   114→   114→   114→            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   115→   115→   115→        )
   116→   116→   116→        logger.info(f'Chrome started with PID {self._process.pid}')
   117→   117→   117→
   118→   118→   118→    async def start(self) -> None:
   119→   119→   119→        """Start Chrome and begin health monitoring.
   120→   120→   120→
   121→   121→   121→        If Chrome is already running on the debug port (e.g. user started
   122→   122→   122→        it manually), we skip launching a new process and just monitor.
   123→   123→   123→        """
   124→   124→   124→        self._running = True
   125→   125→   125→
   126→   126→   126→        # Check if Chrome is already running on the port
   127→   127→   127→        if await self._check_cdp_health():
   128→   128→   128→            logger.info(f'Chrome already running on port {self.debug_port}, skipping launch')
   129→   129→   129→        elif self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
   130→   130→   130→            self._start_chrome_process()
   131→   131→   131→            # Wait for Chrome to be ready
   132→   132→   132→            for _ in range(30):
   133→   133→   133→                if await self._check_cdp_health():
   134→   134→   134→                    logger.info('Chrome CDP endpoint is ready')
   135→   135→   135→                    break
   136→   136→   136→                await asyncio.sleep(1)
   137→   137→   137→            else:
   138→   138→   138→                logger.error('Chrome failed to start within 30 seconds')
   139→   139→   139→        else:
   140→   140→   140→            logger.warning(
   141→   141→   141→                'Chrome auto-launch disabled and no Chrome detected on '
   142→   142→   142→                f'port {self.debug_port}'
   143→   143→   143→            )
   144→   144→   144→
   145→   145→   145→        # Start health check loop
   146→   146→   146→        self._health_task = asyncio.create_task(self._health_loop())
   147→   147→   147→
   148→   148→   148→    async def _health_loop(self) -> None:
   149→   149→   149→        """Periodically check Chrome health and restart if needed."""
   150→   150→   150→        while self._running:
   151→   151→   151→            await asyncio.sleep(10)
   152→   152→   152→            if not self._running:
   153→   153→   153→                break
   154→   154→   154→
   155→   155→   155→            healthy = await self._check_cdp_health()
   156→   156→   156→            if not healthy:
   157→   157→   157→                logger.warning('Chrome health check failed')
   158→   158→   158→                if self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
   159→   159→   159→                    await self._restart_chrome()
   160→   160→   160→
   161→   161→   161→    async def _restart_chrome(self) -> None:
   162→   162→   162→        """Restart Chrome process and reset BrowserManager connection."""
   163→   163→   163→        logger.info('Restarting Chrome...')
   164→   164→   164→
   165→   165→   165→        # Kill existing process if any
   166→   166→   166→        self._kill_process()
   167→   167→   167→
   168→   168→   168→        await asyncio.sleep(2)
   169→   169→   169→
   170→   170→   170→        # Start fresh
   171→   171→   171→        self._start_chrome_process()
   172→   172→   172→
   173→   173→   173→        # Wait for ready
   174→   174→   174→        for _ in range(30):
   175→   175→   175→            if await self._check_cdp_health():
   176→   176→   176→                logger.info('Chrome restarted successfully')
   177→   177→   177→                break
   178→   178→   178→            await asyncio.sleep(1)
   179→   179→   179→        else:
   180→   180→   180→            logger.error('Chrome restart failed')
   181→   181→   181→            return
   182→   182→   182→
   183→   183→   183→        # Reset BrowserManager singleton so it reconnects
   184→   184→   184→        from src.core.browser import BrowserManager
   185→   185→   185→        await BrowserManager.reset_instance()
   186→   186→   186→
   187→   187→   187→    def _kill_process(self) -> None:
   188→   188→   188→        """Terminate the Chrome process."""
   189→   189→   189→        if self._process is None:
   190→   190→   190→            return
   191→   191→   191→
   192→   192→   192→        try:
   193→   193→   193→            self._process.send_signal(signal.SIGTERM)
   194→   194→   194→            try:
   195→   195→   195→                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
   196→   196→   196→            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   197→   197→   197→                self._process.kill()
   198→   198→   198→                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
   199→   199→   199→            logger.info('Chrome process terminated')
   200→   200→   200→        except Exception as e:
   201→   201→   201→            logger.warning(f'Error terminating Chrome: {e}')
   202→   202→   202→        finally:
   203→   203→   203→            self._process = None
   204→   204→   204→

   206→   206→   206→        """Stop health monitoring and terminate Chrome."""
   207→   207→   207→        self._running = False

   210→   210→   210→            self._health_task.cancel()

   213→   213→   213→            except asyncio.CancelledError:
   214→   214→   214→                pass
   215→   215→   215→
   216→   216→   216→        self._kill_process()
   217→   217→   217→        logger.info('ChromeProcessManager stopped')

1→"""Random delay generators for human-like behavior simulation."""
     2→
     3→import math
     4→import random
     5→import asyncio
     6→import time
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from src.utils.config import get_config
     9→
    10→
    11→_session_start: float = time.monotonic()
    12→_action_count: int = 0
    13→
    14→
    15→def reset_session():
    16→    global _session_start, _action_count
    17→    _session_start = time.monotonic()
    18→    _action_count = 0
    19→
    20→
    21→def _lognormal_delay(min_val: float, max_val: float) -> float:
    22→    mid = (min_val + max_val) / 2
    23→    mu = math.log(mid)
    24→    sigma = 0.4
    25→    delay = random.lognormvariate(mu, sigma)
    26→    return max(min_val, min(delay, max_val * 1.5))
    27→
    28→
    29→def _fatigue_multiplier() -> float:
    30→    elapsed_minutes = (time.monotonic() - _session_start) / 60.0
    31→    if elapsed_minutes < 10:
    32→        return 1.0
    33→    factor = 1.0 + 0.006 * min(elapsed_minutes - 10, 100)
    34→    return min(factor, 1.6)
    35→
    36→
    37→def _maybe_distraction_pause() -> float:
    38→    global _action_count
    39→    _action_count += 1
    40→    if _action_count < 5:
    41→        return 0.0
    42→    if random.random() < 0.03:
    43→        pause = random.uniform(5.0, 30.0)
    44→        logger.debug(f"Simulating distraction pause: {pause:.1f}s")
    45→        return pause
    46→    return 0.0
    47→
    48→
    49→def _time_of_day_multiplier() -> float:
    50→    import datetime
    51→    hour = datetime.datetime.now().hour
    52→    if 23 <= hour or hour < 7:
    53→        return 1.3
    54→    elif (10 <= hour <= 12) or (14 <= hour <= 17):
    55→        return 1.0
    56→    else:
    57→        return 1.1
    58→
    59→
    60→def get_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action') -> float:
    61→    config = get_config()
    62→
    63→    delay_ranges = {
    64→        'page_load': (
    65→            config.get('delays.page_load_min', 2),
    66→            config.get('delays.page_load_max', 5)
    67→        ),
    68→        'action': (
    69→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
    70→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
    71→        ),
    72→        'typing': (
    73→            config.get('delays.typing_min', 0.05),
    74→            config.get('delays.typing_max', 0.15)
    75→        ),
    76→        'scroll': (
    77→            config.get('delays.scroll_min', 0.5),
    78→            config.get('delays.scroll_max', 1.5)
    79→        ),
    80→        'message': (
    81→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
    82→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
    83→        ),
    84→    }
    85→
    86→    min_delay, max_delay = delay_ranges.get(delay_type, (0.5, 2.0))
    87→    base_delay = _lognormal_delay(min_delay, max_delay)
    88→
    89→    if delay_type != 'typing':
    90→        base_delay *= _fatigue_multiplier()
    91→        base_delay *= _time_of_day_multiplier()
    92→
    93→    if delay_type in ('action', 'page_load', 'message'):
    94→        base_delay += _maybe_distraction_pause()
    95→
    96→    return base_delay
    97→
    98→
    99→async def async_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action'):
   100→    delay = get_random_delay(delay_type)
   101→    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   102→

1→"""Chrome process lifecycle manager for Web service.
     2→
     3→Manages starting, health-checking, and restarting the Chrome browser
     4→process with CDP enabled. Used by the Web service lifespan to ensure
     5→Chrome is always available for browser automation tasks.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→import shutil
    10→import signal
    11→import subprocess
    12→import sys
    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→from typing import Optional
    15→
    16→import httpx
    17→from loguru import logger
    18→
    19→from src.utils.config import get_config
    20→
    21→
    22→class ChromeProcessManager:
    23→    """Manages the Chrome browser process for the Web service.
    24→
    25→    - Auto-starts Chrome in headed mode with --remote-debugging-port
    26→    - Periodically health-checks via CDP HTTP endpoint
    27→    - Auto-restarts Chrome if it crashes
    28→    - Gracefully terminates Chrome on shutdown
    29→    """
    30→
    31→    def __init__(self):
    32→        self._config = get_config()
    33→        self._process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
    34→        self._health_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
    35→        self._running = False
    36→
    37→    @property
    38→    def debug_port(self) -> int:
    39→        return self._config.get('web.chrome_debug_port', 9222)
    40→
    41→    @property
    42→    def user_data_dir(self) -> str:
    43→        return self._config.get('web.chrome_user_data_dir', 'data/.chrome-profile')
    44→
    45→    @property
    46→    def cdp_url(self) -> str:
    47→        return f'http://127.0.0.1:{self.debug_port}'
    48→
    49→    @property
    50→    def is_chrome_running(self) -> bool:
    51→        return self._process is not None and self._process.poll() is None
    52→
    53→    def _find_chrome_binary(self) -> str:
    54→        """Find Chrome binary on the current platform."""
    55→        if sys.platform == 'darwin':
    56→            candidates = [
    57→                '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome',
    58→                '/Applications/Chromium.app/Contents/MacOS/Chromium',
    59→            ]
    60→        elif sys.platform == 'win32':
    61→            candidates = [
    62→                r'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
    63→                r'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
    64→            ]
    65→        else:
    66→            candidates = [
    67→                'google-chrome',
    68→                'google-chrome-stable',
    69→                'chromium',
    70→                'chromium-browser',
    71→            ]
    72→
    73→        for candidate in candidates:
    74→            if Path(candidate).exists() or shutil.which(candidate):
    75→                return candidate
    76→
    77→        raise RuntimeError(
    78→            'Chrome binary not found. Install Google Chrome or set the path manually.'
    79→        )
    80→
    81→    async def _check_cdp_health(self) -> bool:
    82→        """Check if Chrome CDP endpoint is responsive."""
    83→        try:
    84→            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
    85→                resp = await client.get(
    86→                    f'{self.cdp_url}/json/version',
    87→                    timeout=5.0,
    88→                )
    89→                return resp.status_code == 200
    90→        except Exception:
    91→            return False
    92→
    93→    def _start_chrome_process(self) -> None:
    94→        """Start Chrome with CDP enabled."""
    95→        chrome_binary = self._find_chrome_binary()
    96→        user_data = Path(self.user_data_dir).resolve()
    97→        user_data.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    98→
    99→        args = [
   100→            chrome_binary,
   101→            f'--remote-debugging-port={self.debug_port}',
   102→            f'--user-data-dir={user_data}',
   103→            '--no-first-run',
   104→            '--no-default-browser-check',
   105→            '--disable-background-timer-throttling',
   106→            '--disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows',
   107→            '--disable-renderer-backgrounding',
   108→        ]
   109→
   110→        logger.info(f'Starting Chrome: {" ".join(args[:3])}...')
   111→        self._process = subprocess.Popen(
   112→            args,
   113→            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   114→            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   115→        )
   116→        logger.info(f'Chrome started with PID {self._process.pid}')
   117→
   118→    async def start(self) -> None:
   119→        """Start Chrome and begin health monitoring.
   120→
   121→        If Chrome is already running on the debug port (e.g. user started
   122→        it manually), we skip launching a new process and just monitor.
   123→        """
   124→        self._running = True
   125→
   126→        # Check if Chrome is already running on the port
   127→        if await self._check_cdp_health():
   128→            logger.info(f'Chrome already running on port {self.debug_port}, skipping launch')
   129→        elif self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
   130→            self._start_chrome_process()
   131→            # Wait for Chrome to be ready
   132→            for _ in range(30):
   133→                if await self._check_cdp_health():
   134→                    logger.info('Chrome CDP endpoint is ready')
   135→                    break
   136→                await asyncio.sleep(1)
   137→            else:
   138→                logger.error('Chrome failed to start within 30 seconds')
   139→        else:
   140→            logger.warning(
   141→                'Chrome auto-launch disabled and no Chrome detected on '
   142→                f'port {self.debug_port}'
   143→            )
   144→
   145→        # Start health check loop
   146→        self._health_task = asyncio.create_task(self._health_loop())
   147→
   148→    async def _health_loop(self) -> None:
   149→        """Periodically check Chrome health and restart if needed."""
   150→        while self._running:
   151→            await asyncio.sleep(10)
   152→            if not self._running:
   153→                break
   154→
   155→            healthy = await self._check_cdp_health()
   156→            if not healthy:
   157→                logger.warning('Chrome health check failed')
   158→                if self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
   159→                    await self._restart_chrome()
   160→
   161→    async def _restart_chrome(self) -> None:
   162→        """Restart Chrome process and reset BrowserManager connection."""
   163→        logger.info('Restarting Chrome...')
   164→
   165→        # Kill existing process if any
   166→        self._kill_process()
   167→
   168→        await asyncio.sleep(2)
   169→
   170→        # Start fresh
   171→        self._start_chrome_process()
   172→
   173→        # Wait for ready
   174→        for _ in range(30):
   175→            if await self._check_cdp_health():
   176→                logger.info('Chrome restarted successfully')
   177→                break
   178→            await asyncio.sleep(1)
   179→        else:
   180→            logger.error('Chrome restart failed')
   181→            return
   182→
   183→        # Reset BrowserManager singleton so it reconnects
   184→        from src.core.browser import BrowserManager
   185→        await BrowserManager.reset_instance()
   186→
   187→    def _kill_process(self) -> None:
   188→        """Terminate the Chrome process."""
   189→        if self._process is None:
   190→            return
   191→
   192→        try:
   193→            self._process.send_signal(signal.SIGTERM)
   194→            try:
   195→                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
   196→            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   197→                self._process.kill()
   198→                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
   199→            logger.info('Chrome process terminated')
   200→        except Exception as e:
   201→            logger.warning(f'Error terminating Chrome: {e}')
   202→        finally:
   203→            self._process = None
   204→
   205→    async def stop(self) -> None:
   206→        """Stop health monitoring and terminate Chrome."""
   207→        self._running = False
   208→
   209→        if self._health_task and not self._health_task.done():
   210→            self._health_task.cancel()
   211→            try:
   212→                await self._health_task
   213→            except asyncio.CancelledError:
   214→                pass
   215→
   216→        self._kill_process()
   217→        logger.info('ChromeProcessManager stopped')
   218→

1→"""Templates routes — list and edit message templates."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→from pathlib import Path
     5→
     6→from fastapi import APIRouter, Request, Body
     7→from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse, JSONResponse
     8→
     9→from src.web.app import templates
    10→from src.utils.config import get_config
    11→
    12→router = APIRouter()
    13→
    14→
    15→@router.get("/", response_class=HTMLResponse)
    16→async def templates_page(request: Request):
    17→    """List templates page, or return JSON list if format=json."""
    18→    config = get_config()
    19→    template_names = config.list_templates()
    20→
    21→    # Support JSON format for AJAX requests
    22→    if request.query_params.get("format") == "json":
    23→        result = []
    24→        for name in template_names:
    25→            tmpl = config.get_template(name)
    26→            result.append({
    27→                "name": name,
    28→                "display_name": tmpl.get("name", name),
    29→                "description": tmpl.get("description", ""),
    30→                "variables": tmpl.get("variables", []),
    31→            })
    32→        return JSONResponse(result)
    33→
    34→    template_list = []
    35→    for name in template_names:
    36→        tmpl = config.get_template(name)
    37→        template_list.append({
    38→            "name": name,
    39→            "display_name": tmpl.get("name", name),
    40→            "description": tmpl.get("description", ""),
    41→            "content": tmpl.get("content", ""),
    42→            "variables": tmpl.get("variables", []),
    43→        })
    44→
    45→    return templates.TemplateResponse("templates.html", {
    46→        "request": request,
    47→        "template_list": template_list,
    48→    })
    49→
    50→
    51→@router.put("/{name}")
    52→async def update_template(name: str, body: dict = Body(...)):
    53→    """Update a message template's content."""
    54→    config = get_config()
    55→
    56→    # Verify template exists
    57→    config.get_template(name)
    58→
    59→    # Update in-memory
    60→    config._message_templates[name]["content"] = body.get("content", "")
    61→    if "variables" in body:
    62→        config._message_templates[name]["variables"] = body["variables"]
    63→
    64→    # Persist to disk
    65→    templates_path = Path(config.config_dir) / "message_templates.json"
    66→    with open(templates_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    67→        json.dump(config._message_templates, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    68→
    69→    return {"status": "ok", "message": f"Template '{name}' updated"}
    70→

1→"""Dashboard route — GET /"""
     2→
     3→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     4→
     5→from fastapi import APIRouter, Request
     6→
     7→from src.web.app import chrome_manager, task_queue, templates
     8→from src.models.database import Candidate, Message, SearchHistory, get_db_manager
     9→from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter
    10→
    11→router = APIRouter()
    12→
    13→
    14→@router.get("/")
    15→async def dashboard(request: Request):
    16→    """Main dashboard with status overview and today's stats."""
    17→    db = get_db_manager()
    18→    today_start = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
    19→
    20→    with db.session_scope() as session:
    21→        total_candidates = session.query(Candidate).count()
    22→        total_messages_sent = session.query(Message).filter(Message.status == 'sent').count()
    23→        today_searches = session.query(SearchHistory).filter(
    24→            SearchHistory.executed_at >= today_start
    25→        ).count()
    26→        today_messages = session.query(Message).filter(
    27→            Message.created_at >= today_start,
    28→            Message.status == 'sent',
    29→        ).count()
    30→
    31→    # Chrome status
    32→    chrome_healthy = await chrome_manager._check_cdp_health()
    33→
    34→    # Pipeline status
    35→    pipeline_busy = task_queue.is_busy
    36→    pipeline_task_id = task_queue.current_task_id
    37→
    38→    # Rate limiter status
    39→    try:
    40→        rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
    41→        throttle = rate_limiter.throttle_factor
    42→    except Exception:
    43→        throttle = 1.0
    44→
    45→    return templates.TemplateResponse("dashboard.html", {
    46→        "request": request,
    47→        "chrome_healthy": chrome_healthy,
    48→        "pipeline_busy": pipeline_busy,
    49→        "pipeline_task_id": pipeline_task_id,
    50→        "total_candidates": total_candidates,
    51→        "total_messages_sent": total_messages_sent,
    52→        "today_searches": today_searches,
    53→        "today_messages": today_messages,
    54→        "throttle_factor": throttle,
    55→    })
    56→
